title: Debug a SwiftUI view that won't update
description: Trace why a SwiftUI view ignores a change by checking the state ownership and observation wrappers.
body: Steps: 1. Identify the data the view should react to and where it lives. 2. Check the wrapper: is value state @State, is a model @StateObject/@ObservedObject, are its mutable fields @Published? 3. Confirm the mutated property is actually the observed source, not a copy or a plain stored property. 4. Verify bindings (@Binding) propagate writes back to the owner. 5. Check the view isn't capturing a stale value or recreating its @StateObject. Rollback point: before restructuring ownership (can change identity/lifecycle). Validation: mutating the source re-renders the view automatically; if the UI updates only after a manual nudge, the state is not properly observed — fix the source of truth, not the symptom.
